back_squat.beginner: Push through the middle of your feet as you stand up, and keep your chest lifted.

front_squat.beginner: Keep your chest up and your elbows pointing forward and slightly up. Lower yourself down slowly and with control.

trap_bar_dl.beginner: Stand up straight, then imagine pushing the ground away with your feet to lift the weight.

rdl_db.beginner: Bend at your hips like you're trying to touch a wall behind you, keeping a slight bend in your knees.

bulgarian_split.beginner: Try to keep the front of your lower leg (shin) pointing straight up and down as you squat.

walking_lunge.beginner: Take a big step forward, and move smoothly and slowly through the exercise.

goblet_squat.beginner: This exercise is a good way to practice squatting down as low as you can comfortably go.

reverse_lunge_bw.beginner: Take a big step backward, then lower your back knee toward the floor. Push through the heel of your front foot to stand back up.

single_leg_glute_bridge.beginner: At the highest point of the movement, really squeeze your butt muscles together.

step_up_bw.beginner: Stand in front of a strong chair or bench. Step up with one foot, and use that leg to lift your body, not your back foot.

cossack_squat.beginner: Bend your knees and hips to move your weight to one side, keeping both feet flat on the floor. Then, shift to the other side.

single_leg_rdl_bw.beginner: Bend at your hips while keeping your back straight, and extend your free leg straight behind you.

wall_sit.beginner: Sit against the wall as if you're in a chair, with your knees and hips bent at right angles. Stay in this position.

depth_jump.beginner: Step off the box, then jump as high as you can right when your feet hit the ground. Focus on making each jump good, not just doing many.

box_jump.beginner: When you step off the box, take a controlled step down instead of jumping.

broad_jump.beginner: Jump as far as you can, then land softly with your knees bent.

approach_jump.beginner: Take three steps like you're about to spike a volleyball, and jump as high as you can each time.

tuck_jump.beginner: Jump up and bring your knees towards your chest. Try to land softly and get back up quickly.

pogo_jump.beginner: Keep your ankles firm and spring up quickly from the floor.

lateral_bound.beginner: Push off the ground with the leg that's on the outside of your jump, then land softly and hold your balance.

skater_jump.beginner: When you land, try to be steady and stop your movement smoothly.

db_row.beginner: Pull the dumbbell up by bringing your elbow towards your hip, like you're tucking it in.

barbell_row.beginner: Bend at your hips until your upper body is about halfway down, keeping your back straight and flat.

face_pull.beginner: This exercise is important for keeping your shoulders healthy, especially if you play sports where you lift your arms overhead a lot.

inverted_row.beginner: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Pull your chest towards the bar.

plank.beginner: Tighten your butt muscles and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, like a board.

pallof_press.beginner: Keep your body still and don't let the band pull you to the side.

hanging_leg_raise.beginner: Try to lift your legs up smoothly, without letting your body rock back and forth.

med_ball_slam.beginner: Reach the ball all the way up, then throw it down to the floor with all your might.

rotational_throw.beginner: Use your hips to push the ball away from your body.

calf_raise.beginner: At the highest point of the movement, hold still for a moment before lowering back down.

nordic_curl.beginner: Slowly lower yourself down, bending at your knees. This helps prevent injuries. Make sure your feet are held securely.

copenhagen.beginner: This exercise helps make the muscles on the inside of your thighs stronger and more able to handle stress.

ankle_hops.beginner: Jump up lightly and quickly, trying to spend as little time touching the ground as possible.

cone_drill.beginner: Step firmly on your outside foot, then push off powerfully in the opposite direction.

defensive_slide.beginner: Keep your hips down as you slide, and don't cross one foot over the other.
